module-0-inventory-intake-batch.label: Module 0 Project: New Inventory Intake
module-0-inventory-intake-batch.summary: Add a five-item approved inventory batch through five cumulative INSERT steps.

ci_module_0_step2.title: Record an Item with No Notes

ci_module_0_step4.title: Add Two Items as One Batch

ci_module_0_step1.title: Add the First Approved Item
ci_module_0_step1.prompt:
The fulfillment team has approved the first item for its new packing station. In `operations.sql`, insert `id 101` with name `Desk Organizer`, category `Office`, price `12.99`, status `active`, `is_test = 0`, notes `New stock`, and `last_updated = '2026-02-10'`. Use an explicit column list, then verify the new row with `SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE id = 101`.

Write the approved `INSERT` statement in `operations.sql`.

In `query.sql`, return `*` from `inventory_items` for rows matching `id = 101`.
ci_module_0_step1.hint: Apply the narrowly scoped change in operations.sql, then write query.sql so the Results tab proves the final state.

help.concept: Keep the approved data change in `operations.sql` and the evidence in `query.sql`. The exercise supplies the existing table and starting rows so you can focus on the mutation.
help.hint_1: Apply the narrowly scoped change in operations.sql, then write query.sql so the Results tab proves the final state.
help.hint_2: In `query.sql`, return `*` from `inventory_items` for rows matching `id = 101`.

ci_module_0_step1.starterCode: -- Apply the approved data change for "Add the First Approved Item" below.
ci_module_0_step1.solutionCode:
-- Insert the first approved item
INSERT INTO inventory_items (id, name, category, price, status, is_test, notes, last_updated)
VALUES
  (101, 'Desk Organizer', 'Office', 12.99, 'active', 0, 'New stock', '2026-02-10');

operations_sql.content: -- Apply the approved data change for "Add the First Approved Item" below.

query_sql.content: -- Return the verification result required for "Add the First Approved Item" below.

VALUES

query_sql.content:
-- Verify the insertion
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE id = 101;

ci_module_0_step2.title: Record an Item with No Notes
ci_module_0_step2.prompt:
A second approved item has no notes on the intake sheet. Keep the project moving by inserting `id 102`, `Cable Labels`, category `Office`, price `5.99`, status `active`, `is_test = 0`, SQL `NULL` for `notes`, and `last_updated = '2026-02-11'`. Verify only row `102` after the insert.

Write the approved `INSERT` statement in `operations.sql`.

In `query.sql`, return `*` from `inventory_items` for rows matching `id = 102`.
ci_module_0_step2.hint: Apply the narrowly scoped change in operations.sql, then write query.sql so the Results tab proves the final state.

ci_module_0_step3.title: Use the Approved Defaults
ci_module_0_step3.prompt:
The Shipping Pouch should receive the database's approved defaults instead of hard-coded values. Insert `id 103`, name `Shipping Pouch`, category `Office`, price `3.49`, `is_test = 0`, and notes `Durable material`. Omit `status` and `last_updated` from the column list, then select row `103` to confirm the insert.

Write the approved `INSERT` statement in `operations.sql`.

In `query.sql`, return `*` from `inventory_items` for rows matching `id = 103`.
ci_module_0_step3.hint: Apply the narrowly scoped change in operations.sql, then write query.sql so the Results tab proves the final state.

ci_module_0_step4.title: Add Two Items as One Batch
ci_module_0_step4.prompt:
Two small packing supplies were approved together and should be recorded as one batch. Use one multi-row `INSERT` to add `id 104`, `Packing Tape`, price `2.99`, notes `Strong adhesive`, and `id 105`, `Label Roll`, price `1.99`, notes `Easy to peel`. Both rows use category `Office`, status `active`, `is_test = 0`, and `last_updated = '2026-02-12'`. Verify IDs `104` and `105`.

Write the approved `INSERT` statement in `operations.sql`.

In `query.sql`, return `*` from `inventory_items` for rows matching `id IN (104, 105)`, ordered by `id`.
ci_module_0_step4.hint: Apply the narrowly scoped change in operations.sql, then write query.sql so the Results tab proves the final state.

help.concept: Keep the approved data change in `operations.sql` and the evidence in `query.sql`. The exercise supplies the existing table and starting rows so you can focus on the mutation.
help.hint_1: Apply the narrowly scoped change in operations.sql, then write query.sql so the Results tab proves the final state.
help.hint_2: In `query.sql`, return `*` from `inventory_items` for rows matching `id IN (104, 105)`, ordered by `id`.

ci_module_0_step5.title: Finish the Intake Handoff
ci_module_0_step5.prompt:
The intake script is ready for an operations handoff. Return every column from `inventory_items` and sort the complete inventory by `id`, so the reviewer can confirm the five new records with IDs `101` through `105` alongside the rows that already existed.

In `query.sql`, return `*` from `inventory_items`, ordered by `id`.

Keep the completed work in `operations.sql` unchanged while you finish this step.
ci_module_0_step5.hint: Leave the setup files unchanged and write the requested result-returning statement in query.sql.

help.concept: Keep the approved data change in `operations.sql` and the evidence in `query.sql`. The exercise supplies the existing table and starting rows so you can focus on the mutation.
help.hint_1: Leave the setup files unchanged and write the requested result-returning statement in query.sql.

ci_module_0_step5.starterCode: -- Return the verification result required for "Finish the Intake Handoff" below.
ci_module_0_step5.solutionCode:
-- Verify all new items and existing inventory
SELECT * FROM inventory_items ORDER BY id;

sketch-module-0-intro.title: New Inventory Intake Project
sketch-module-0-intro.bodyMarkdown:
A small makers-market fulfillment team is opening a new packing station. You are the junior data operations analyst responsible for turning an approved five-item intake sheet into a safe, reviewable database change.

The project begins with one item and grows into a complete intake batch. You will practice explicit column lists, SQL `NULL`, table defaults, and one multi-row insert while preserving every record that already exists.

Your deliverable is a two-file intake workspace:

- `operations.sql` contains the cumulative `INSERT` statements for IDs 101 through 105.
- `query.sql` returns the newly added rows and finishes with the full inventory ordered by `id`.

Each step carries the previous approved work forward, so the final workspace reads as one coherent operational script rather than a collection of unrelated answers.
